title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Wakisugi explains the complex series of murders orchestrated by Mistuho, detailing how she used literary themes to mask her crimes. In the Ito-Uma case, Mistuho originally intended to theme the crime after "The American Gun Mystery" using a specific record and a horse. However, when Ito accidentally created a locked room, Mistuho improvised by changing the theme to "The Chinese Orange Mystery." To match this new theme, she threw orange seeds through a small hole in a marble door, decapitated Ito, placed his head on a hat rack, and left bloodstains on the carpet. The record intended for the original theme remained in Uma's locked room because Murata could not enter.

Regarding the Akatsuki murder, Akiba avoided suspicion by hiding stolen items in her skirt, utilizing a technique used by American pickpocket gangs. To kill Jouma, Akiba wore oversized clothing and placed Tamekichi's head on her own head to mimic a scene from an Aeschylus tragedy, which lured Yuzu into opening Tamekichi's coffin. In the case of Fujiko, Mistuho killed McArthur, the son of Tomioka, after he attempted to team up with Fujiko to claim Tomioka's property. Because McArthur wore a top hat, Mistuho adapted the theme to "The Mystery of the Roman Hat," a theme originally intended for Yūen.

Wakisugi reveals that Mistuho's motive is a twisted Oedipus complex. Disappointed by the reality of her father, Kanayanagi, Mistuho sought to transform Kaji into a "God." Transitioning from a believer to a figure resembling the Virgin Mary, she aimed to eliminate other Mikumo family members to grant Kaji absolute power and bloodline legitimacy.
